## [00:00 - 02:27]

Okay, this is all about the trial close. The trial is a sort of test. We're not talking about a jury trial. We're talking about you testing something. This can be done anywhere, but we want to focus here towards the end of the product demonstration and moving toward the write-up. Trial close is designed to give you a sense of what they're thinking and feeling about your product or service and how to adjust your presentation so you can actually make the sale. This is known by all salespeople and underutilized by most. The trial close is an attempt to start closing the sale before you're even in the close. It's a test to see how near we are. You're taking temperature, basically. Trial close are valuable. They're low risk. Ask for an opinion or evaluation where the close asks for a decision. You understand? The trial close asks for an opinion. The close asks for a decision. Your trial close is a measurement, not a commitment. We're going to measure where they're at. The trial close is going to tell you where to adjust your presentation, particularly in the close. The trial close is the best, single best diagnostic tool that you have for finding out where people are at.
